1	---
2	name: blueprint-conventions
3	description: The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
4	---
5	
6	Blueprints are a LaTeX subset (the `leanblueprint` dialect) parsed into the DAG.
7	Write timeless mathematics — a standalone document, not a project journal.
8	
9	## Environments
10	
11	Each declaration is an amsthm-style environment; the recognized ones are
12	`theorem`, `lemma`, `proposition`, `corollary`, `definition`, `remark`,
13	`example`, `notation`, `convention`, and `proof`. A statement environment plus
14	its following `proof` environment form one node's content.
15	
16	## Per-node macros
17	
18	- `\label{id}` — a stable id (the DAG node key).
19	- `\lean{Name}` — the Lean declaration this node formalizes. Keep it 1-to-1: one
20	  blueprint node ↔ one Lean declaration, signatures matching.
21	- `\uses{a,b}` — dependencies on other nodes' labels; these drive the DAG edges.
22	  Add an edge instead of re-explaining a dependency in prose. Put `\uses` in the
23	  statement for definitional deps and in the `proof` for deps used only in the
24	  proof.
25	- `\source{slug:page-0001}` — reference anchors for UI popups and audit trails.
26	  Use page-level ids from `references/<slug>/tex/page-0001.tex` or an equivalent
27	  manifest entry. Multiple anchors may be comma-separated.
28	- `\leanok` — the Lean is written and checked. Honest only when its Lean is
29	  actually checked *and* its dependencies are too. On a `proof`, it means the
30	  proof is formalized; on a statement, the signature is formalized.
31	- `\mathlibok` — the declaration is already available in mathlib (no
32	  formalization needed; treat as a leaf).
33	
34	## House style
35	
36	- **Pure mathematics only.** No Lean tactics, typeclass/implementation notes, or
37	  semi-Lean pseudocode. No project history ("since iter N", "our failed route")
38	  and no conversational filler. If a Lean helper exists with no math counterpart,
39	  write its LaTeX statement so the 1-to-1 correspondence holds.
40	- **Complete proofs, not sketches.** A node's `proof` is a real, rigorous
41	  mathematical proof a reader could check — not a hand-wave or a TODO. The way to
42	  keep it short is to *split*, not to abbreviate: if a proof is long or a node
43	  grows past a short paragraph, break out the hard step as its own `\uses`-linked
44	  lemma (down to sentence-sized lemmas if needed) so each node stays small and
45	  its proof stays complete.
46	- **One declaration's worth per node.** Don't restate Lean source as prose.
47	- **Cite by source link, not quote comments.** Add `\source{...}` to the
48	  relevant node. Keep the retrieved/transcribed page text in `references/` (for
49	  example `references/<slug>/tex/page-0042.tex`) and let the UI expose it; do not
50	  paste verbatim source quotes into `% QUOTE` comments inside the blueprint.
51

1	---
2	name: leandag
3	description: Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
4	---
5	
6	The DAG links blueprint nodes by `\uses` edges. An edge `source -> target` means
7	*target depends on source*. The engine records each node's `\lean` link,
8	`proved`/`leanok` status, effort metrics, and Lean source metadata.
9	
10	## Keep It Fresh
11	
12	- Run the project build after Lean/blueprint edits so generated Lean artifacts and
13	  imported libraries are current.
14	- Run `horizon blueprint` after blueprint or Lean declaration changes; it writes
15	  `.archon-horizon/blueprints/<project>.json` for the dashboard and cached DAG
16	  consumers.
17	- Use `horizon leandag` for live inspection when deciding what to prove next.
18	
19	## `horizon leandag` (preferred)
20	
21	- `horizon leandag` — per-project summary: nodes, edges, proved count, and any
22	  isolated nodes (no edge in or out — usually a blueprint bug to fix).
23	- `horizon leandag -p <project> --node <label>` — one node's direct deps, full
24	  transitive cone, and reverse deps.
25	- `horizon leandag -p a -p b --union` — merge projects by declaration/node id.
26	  Same-id nodes are combined; divergent non-signature metadata is tolerated, but
27	  a Lean signature mismatch is an error to investigate.
28	- `horizon leandag -p a -p b --intersect` — only shared declaration/node ids,
29	  with the same signature check.
30	- Add `--json` for machine-readable output.
31	
32	A node is "ready" when all dependencies are proved. Prefer ready nodes when
33	choosing what to attempt next.
34	
35	## Cached JSON
36	
37	`horizon blueprint` writes each project's DAG to
38	`.archon-horizon/blueprints/<project>.json` (`nodes`, `edges`, `dangling`) for
39	the dashboard. Under the leandag engine, `\uses` are resolved at build/cache
40	time, so `dangling` is empty there; the parser fallback still populates it. To
41	check for orphan/unresolved nodes, use `horizon leandag` rather than relying only
42	on `dangling`.
43

1	---
2	name: references
3	description: The shared workspace-root references/ library and its manifest.yaml — how to find, read, and cite original sources with \source{...}. Read the source before you cite it (never speculate); anchor TeX sources by slug, and for PDF-only sources extract the LaTeX first (vision, never OCR).
4	---
5	
6	`references/` is a **shared library at the workspace root** — one library for
7	every project — holding the original source material a blueprint is formalised
8	from (papers, books, notes). It is indexed by `references/manifest.yaml`. Per-
9	project files live under each project's directory; `references/` does not — it is
10	always at the workspace root, and `references/**` is writable from any agent.
11	
12	A blueprint node derived from a source carries a `\source{...}` anchor. Those
13	anchors are the audit trail from a formal statement back to where it came from,
14	so they must be **true**, not plausible.
15	
16	## The one rule: read before you cite
17	
18	**Never add a `\source{...}` anchor to a source you have not actually read.** Do
19	not infer a citation from a title, a topic, a theorem number, or your own prior
20	knowledge of the result. An anchor asserts "this exact statement/proof appears
21	here" — if you haven't opened the referenced material and confirmed it, you don't
22	know that, and a wrong anchor is worse than none (it sends the next reader/agent
23	to the wrong place and launders a guess as provenance).
24	
25	Concretely, before writing `\source{...}`:
26	1. Confirm the source exists in `references/manifest.yaml`. If it isn't there,
27	   get it in first (see below) — don't cite a slug that doesn't resolve.
28	2. Open the actual content — the TeX, or the transcribed page — and confirm the
29	   statement/proof you're anchoring is really there.
30	3. Only then write the anchor.
31	
32	## Anchor forms — pick by what the source actually is
33	
34	The manifest's `read` field for each entry tells you which case you're in.
35	
36	- **The source has LaTeX** (arXiv e-print, an open `.tex`, etc.): read the TeX
37	  directly and cite the source as a whole — `\source{slug}`. Do **not** invent a
38	  `page-NNNN`; a TeX source has no rendered pages, so a page number would be
39	  fabricated. If the manifest documents stable sub-ids for the source (a section
40	  or label id), you may use `\source{slug:<id>}`, but only an id that exists.
41	- **The source is PDF-only** (no usable TeX): cite the transcribed page,
42	  `\source{slug:page-NNNN}`, where `page-NNNN` is a real file under
43	  `references/<slug>/tex/page-NNNN.tex` that you have read. If that page hasn't
44	  been transcribed yet, transcribe it first (below) — don't cite a page that
45	  doesn't exist on disk.
46	
47	Multiple anchors may be comma-separated: `\source{slug:page-0042, other:page-0007}`.
48	
49	## Getting a source into the library
50	
51	Don't fetch and stash sources ad hoc. Spawn the **`reference-retriever`** subagent
52	(by name, through your engine's native subagent mechanism) with whatever you have
53	— topic, arXiv ID, DOI, title, URL, target page range. It downloads the original
54	(preferring TeX, keeping the PDF), verifies the file is real (not a paywall/login
55	page named `.pdf`), organises it under `references/<slug>/`, and registers it in
56	`references/manifest.yaml`. Wait for it, then cite from what it wrote.
57	
58	## Extracting the LaTeX from a PDF-only source
59	
60	When there is no reliable TeX and you need the actual math (to read it, or to cite
61	a page), **extract it with the proper tool — don't eyeball the PDF and paraphrase.**
62	The extraction path is the **`page-transcriber`** subagent, which renders each page
63	to an image and **vision-transcribes** it into one self-contained
64	`references/<slug>/tex/page-NNNN.tex` per page.
65	
66	- **Vision, never OCR.** OCR mangles math (subscripts, operators, alignment);
67	  reading the rendered page image with a vision model preserves it. This is a hard
68	  rule of the transcription flow.
69	- **A small vision model is usually enough.** Faithful page transcription doesn't
70	  need a frontier model — a cheap vision-capable model (e.g. Haiku) running in the
71	  background typically suffices, which is what the config is for:
72	
73	  ```yaml
74	  references:
75	    transcription:
76	      harness: <optional harness>
77	      model: <cheap vision-capable model, e.g. a Haiku-class model>
78	  ```
79	
80	  Don't pick the model inside the prompt; pin it in config so every transcription
81	  uses the same cheap vision model. When unset it falls back to the subagent/Ground
82	  harness.
83	- **Keep each call ≤5 pages.** Longer ranges lose accuracy; split a big range
84	  across several `page-transcriber` calls.
85	
86	## Reading transcriptions correctly
87	
88	- A transcribed page is `references/<slug>/tex/page-NNNN.tex` where `NNNN` is the
89	  **PDF page index**, which often differs from the printed page number. The
90	  manifest entry's `read` field records the `page_offset` and any fallback that
91	  actually worked — respect it when mapping "Theorem 3.4 on p. 12" to a file.
92	- Read the whole page file before citing it; a page can hold several results.
93	
94	## The manifest at a glance
95	
96	One entry per source (the `reference-retriever` owns the full schema):
97	
98	```yaml
99	references:
100	  - slug: <slug>
101	    title: <title>
102	    source: <arXiv ID / DOI / URL>
103	    status: ok            # or: not_found
104	    files:
105	      - { path: <slug>/<slug>.pdf, format: pdf }
106	      - { path: <slug>/source.tex, format: tex }        # if TeX obtained
107	      - { path: <slug>/tex/page-0042.tex, format: tex } # if transcribed
108	    read: "Read TeX when present; else page anchors slug:page-NNNN. page_offset: ..."
109	```
110	
111	If a source can't be found and verified, it's recorded `status: not_found` — treat
112	that as "no citation available," and write no math attributed to it.
113	
114	Related skills: `blueprint-conventions` for where `\source{...}` sits among the
115	other blueprint annotations, and `leandag` for how a node's `sources` feed the DAG.
116

6	import Mathlib

10	/-!
11	# Universal property of the flat-locus stratification (Nitsure §4, `n = 0`)
12	
13	This file proves the *representability* layer of the flat-locus
14	stratification and assembles it into the universal theorem
15	`AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification_universal` (sorry-free): a
16	morphism `q : T ⟶ X` whose pulled-back module is flat of constant fiber
17	rank `e` factors uniquely through the rank-`e` stratum
18	`stratumι : stratum G e hcov ⟶ X` of
19	`AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.EntryIdealStratum`, the stratum itself carries a
20	flat pullback, and over a noetherian base the finitely many rank strata
21	form the universal flattening stratification — parts (i)+(ii) of the
22	special case `n = 0` of the flattening-stratification theorem in
23	[Nitsure §4].
24	
25	## Contents
26	
27	* §1 — bridges: matrix presentations of pullback section modules
28	  (`exists_matrixPresentation_pullback_sections`, via the Lane F section
29	  formula `pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv`), transport of
30	  flatness between the natural and the `appLE`-restriction module structures
31	  (`flat_compHom_congr`), and extraction of section flatness from
32	  `CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 T)` (`flat_sections_of_coherentSheafFlat_id`,
33	  `coherentSheafFlat_id_of_charts`).
34	* §2 — the rank dictionary: `rankAtStalk` of an affine section module
35	  computes the `pointRank` (`rankAtStalk_sections_eq_pointRank`), and the
36	  point rank of a pullback module is the point rank at the image point
37	  (`pointRank_pullback`; no flatness hypotheses, via
38	  `Ideal.fiberRank_baseChange`).
39	* §3 — Stage D1: the kernel bound `strataData_le_ker` (a flat
40	  constant-rank-`e` pullback kills the strata ideal) and the unique
41	  factorization `existsUnique_stratumLift` through the closed immersion
42	  `stratumι` (`IsClosedImmersion.lift`).
43	* §4 — Stage D2: the stratum pullback is flat over the stratum
44	  (`coherentSheafFlat_stratum`): on chart preimages the section module is
45	  presented with vanishing relation matrix, hence free.
46	* §5 — chart supply on a locally noetherian base
47	  (`exists_presentationChart_mem`, via the Nakayama prolongation) and the
48	  chart locus `chartLocus` with its rank bounds.
49	* §6 — the rank stratum `rankStratum`/`rankStratumι` over the chart locus:
50	  a locally closed immersion with support exactly the rank-`e` locus
51	  (`mem_range_rankStratumι_iff`) and flat pullback
52	  (`coherentSheafFlat_rankStratum`); flatness is stable under arbitrary
53	  pullback (`coherentSheafFlat_id_pullback`).
54	* §7 — the rank bound on a noetherian base (`exists_pointRank_le`) and
55	  openness of the rank fibers of a flat pullback
56	  (`isOpen_pointRank_pullback_eq`).
57	* §8 — unique factorization of a constant-rank flat morphism through the
58	  rank stratum (`existsUnique_factor_rankStratum`).
59	* §9 — the universal theorem `flatLocusStratification_universal`.
60	
61	Blueprint chapter: `Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`,
62	§`sec:flatstrat_universal`.
63	Source: [Nitsure], §4, proof of the flattening-stratification theorem,
64	special case `n = 0` (`references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/`
65	`nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex`, L1849–L1885).
66	-/

68	universe u
69	
70	open TensorProduct CategoryTheory
71	
72	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
73	
74	open Module
75	
76	namespace Scheme.Modules
77	
78	/-! ## §1 Presentations of pullback sections and flatness bridges -/
79	
80	section PullbackPresentation
81	
82	variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X) (G : X.Modules) [G.IsQuasicoherent]
83	
84	/-- Elementwise fibre-side `appLE` coherence: restricting the image of
85	`appLE` equals `appLE` into the smaller open. -/
86	private lemma appLE_res_apply' {W W' : Y.Opens} {V : X.Opens}
87	    (e : W ≤ g ⁻¹ᵁ V) (h : W' ≤ W) (u : Γ(X, V)) :
88	    (Y.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom ((g.appLE V W e).hom u) =
89	      (g.appLE V W' (h.trans e)).hom u := by
90	  have h1 := congrArg (fun (φ : Γ(X, V) ⟶ Γ(Y, W')) => φ.hom u)
91	    (Scheme.Hom.appLE_map (f := g) e (homOfLE h).op)
92	  simpa only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply] using h1
93	
94	/-- Elementwise base-side `appLE` coherence: `appLE` of a restricted base
95	section equals `appLE` from the larger base open. -/
96	private lemma appLE_base_res_apply' {V' V : X.Opens} {W : Y.Opens}
97	    (h : V' ≤ V) (e' : W ≤ g ⁻¹ᵁ V') (e : W ≤ g ⁻¹ᵁ V) (u : Γ(X, V)) :
98	    (g.appLE V' W e').hom ((X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom u) =
99	      (g.appLE V W e).hom u := by
100	  have h1 := congrArg (fun (φ : Γ(X, V) ⟶ Γ(Y, W)) => φ.hom u)
101	    (Scheme.Hom.map_appLE (f := g) e' (homOfLE h).op)
102	  simpa only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply] using h1
103	
104	/-- **Matrix presentations pull back to section modules** (the geometric
105	base-change of a local presentation, [Nitsure §4]: "the pulled-back
106	sequence is exact by right-exactness of tensor products").  For a morphism
107	`g : Y ⟶ X`, an affine `V ⊆ X`, an affine `W ⊆ g⁻¹V` and an `ee`-generator
108	presentation `P` of `Γ(G, V)`, the section module `Γ(g^*G, W)` admits an
109	`ee`-generator presentation whose relation matrix is the image of that of
110	`P` under `g.appLE`. -/
111	theorem exists_matrixPresentation_pullback_sections
112	    {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) {W : Y.Opens} (hW : IsAffineOpen W)
113	    (e : W ≤ g ⁻¹ᵁ V) {ee mm : ℕ}
114	    (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V) Γ(G, V) ee mm) :
115	    ∃ P' : MatrixPresentation Γ(Y, W)
116	      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj G, W) ee mm,
117	      P'.relMatrix = P.relMatrix.map (g.appLE V W e).hom := by
118	  letI : Algebra Γ(X, V) Γ(Y, W) := (g.appLE V W e).hom.toAlgebra
119	  letI : Module Γ(X, V) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj G, W) :=
120	    Module.compHom _ (g.appLE V W e).hom
121	  obtain ⟨⟨eqv, -⟩⟩ :=
122	    pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv g G hW hV e
123	  refine ⟨(P.baseChange Γ(Y, W)).congr eqv, ?_⟩
124	  rw [Module.MatrixPresentation.congr_relMatrix,
125	    Module.MatrixPresentation.baseChange_relMatrix,
126	    RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra]
127	
128	end PullbackPresentation
129	
130	section FlatBridge
131	
132	/-- Transport of `Module.Flat` between the canonical module structure and a
133	`Module.compHom` structure along a pointwise-identity ring endomorphism
134	(the `appLE` of the identity morphism is the identity restriction, but not
135	definitionally the identity ring hom). -/
136	private lemma flat_compHom_congr {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type u}
137	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] (f : R →+* R) (hf : ∀ r, f r = r) :
138	    (letI : Module R M := Module.compHom M f
139	     Module.Flat R M) ↔ Module.Flat R M := by
140	  have h : (Module.compHom M f : Module R M) = ‹Module R M› :=
141	    Module.ext' _ _ fun r m => by
142	      show f r • m = r • m
143	      rw [hf r]
144	  exact (congrArg (fun i : Module R M => @Module.Flat R M _ _ i) h).to_iff
145	
146	variable {T : Scheme.{u}}
147	
148	/-- The `appLE` of the identity morphism at equal opens is pointwise the
149	identity. -/
150	private lemma id_appLE_apply {W : T.Opens}
151	    (e : W ≤ (𝟙 T : T ⟶ T) ⁻¹ᵁ W) (r : Γ(T, W)) :
152	    ((𝟙 T : T ⟶ T).appLE W W e).hom r = r := by
153	  rw [Scheme.id_appLE]
154	  exact presheaf_map_self (X := T) e r
155	
156	/-- **Extraction of section flatness from flatness over the identity**: if
157	`G` is flat over `T` via `𝟙 T` (the encoding of "the coherent sheaf `G` is
158	flat" in `Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat`), then the sections over every affine
159	open form a flat module over the section ring. -/
160	theorem flat_sections_of_coherentSheafFlat_id {G : T.Modules}
161	    (h : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 T) G) {W : T.Opens}
162	    (hW : IsAffineOpen W) : Module.Flat Γ(T, W) Γ(G, W) :=
163	  (flat_compHom_congr ((𝟙 T : T ⟶ T).appLE W W (le_refl W)).hom
164	    (id_appLE_apply _)).mp (h hW hW (le_refl W))
165	
166	/-- **Flatness over the identity from an affine cover of flat sections**:
167	if the sections of `G` over each member of an affine open cover of `T` are
168	flat over the respective section rings, then `G` is flat over `T` via
169	`𝟙 T`. -/
170	theorem coherentSheafFlat_id_of_charts (G : T.Modules) [G.IsQuasicoherent]
171	    {ι : Type u} (Wc : ι → T.Opens) (hWc : ∀ j, IsAffineOpen (Wc j))
172	    (hcover : ∀ y : T, ∃ j, y ∈ Wc j)
173	    (hflat : ∀ j, Module.Flat Γ(T, Wc j) Γ(G, Wc j)) :
174	    Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 T) G := by
175	  intro U hU V hV eV
176	  exact flat_section_of_affine_cover (𝟙 T) G Wc hWc Wc hWc
177	    (fun j => le_refl _) hcover
178	    (fun j => (flat_compHom_congr
179	      ((𝟙 T : T ⟶ T).appLE (Wc j) (Wc j) (le_refl _)).hom
180	      (id_appLE_apply _)).mpr (hflat j)) hU hV eV
181	
182	end FlatBridge
183	
184	/-! ## §2 The rank dictionary -/
185	
186	section RankDictionary
187	
188	variable {T : Scheme.{u}} (G : T.Modules) [G.IsQuasicoherent]
189	
190	/-- A prime of the section ring of an affine open is the prime ideal of
191	the corresponding point. -/
192	private lemma primeIdealOf_fromSpec {W : T.Opens} (hW : IsAffineOpen W)
193	    (p : PrimeSpectrum Γ(T, W)) (hmem : (hW.fromSpec p : T) ∈ W) :
194	    hW.primeIdealOf ⟨hW.fromSpec p, hmem⟩ = p := by
195	  have hinj : Function.Injective hW.fromSpec :=
196	    hW.fromSpec.isOpenEmbedding.injective
197	  exact hinj (by rw [hW.fromSpec_primeIdealOf])
198	
199	/-- **The stalk rank of an affine section module computes the point rank**:
200	for a quasi-coherent `G` with flat, finite sections `Γ(G, W)` over an
201	affine `W`, the rank of `Γ(G, W)` at a prime `p` is the point rank of `G`
202	at the corresponding point `fromSpec p` [Nitsure §4: flatness plus finite
203	presentation make the fibre dimension compute the local rank]. -/
204	theorem rankAtStalk_sections_eq_pointRank {W : T.Opens}
205	    (hW : IsAffineOpen W) [Module.Flat Γ(T, W) Γ(G, W)]
206	    [Module.Finite Γ(T, W) Γ(G, W)] (p : PrimeSpectrum Γ(T, W)) :
207	    Module.rankAtStalk Γ(G, W) p = pointRank T G (hW.fromSpec p) := by
208	  have hmem : (hW.fromSpec p : T) ∈ W := by
209	    have h0 : (hW.fromSpec p : T) ∈ Set.range hW.fromSpec :=
210	      Set.mem_range_self p
211	    rwa [hW.range_fromSpec] at h0
212	  rw [Module.rankAtStalk_eq,
213	    pointRank_eq_chartFiberRank G (V := ⟨W, hW⟩) _ hmem]
214	  show p.asIdeal.fiberRank Γ(G, W) = chartFiberRank G _ hmem
215	  rw [chartFiberRank]
216	  exact Ideal.fiberRank_congr_ideal
217	    (congrArg PrimeSpectrum.asIdeal (primeIdealOf_fromSpec hW p hmem)).symm
218	
219	end RankDictionary
220	
221	section PointRankPullback
222	
223	variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X) (G : X.Modules) [G.IsQuasicoherent]
224	
225	/-- **The point rank of a pullback module is the point rank at the image
226	point** (no flatness or finiteness hypotheses; the geometric form of
227	`Ideal.fiberRank_baseChange`): for any morphism `g : Y ⟶ X` and
228	quasi-coherent `G` on `X`,
229	`pointRank (g^*G) y = pointRank G (g y)`. -/
230	theorem pointRank_pullback (y : Y) :
231	    haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom g G ‹_›
232	    pointRank Y ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj G) y = pointRank X G (g y) := by
233	  haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom g G ‹_›
234	  -- affine charts around the image point and the point
235	  obtain ⟨V, hV, hgyV, -⟩ :=
236	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (x := g y) (U := ⊤) trivial
237	  have hyV : y ∈ g ⁻¹ᵁ V := hgyV
238	  obtain ⟨W, hW, hyW, hWle⟩ :=
239	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (x := y) (U := g ⁻¹ᵁ V) hyV
240	  -- both sides through the two charts
241	  rw [pointRank_eq_chartFiberRank _ (V := ⟨W, hW⟩) y hyW,
242	    pointRank_eq_chartFiberRank G (V := ⟨V, hV⟩) (g y) hgyV]
243	  -- the section formula for the pullback module over the affine pair
244	  letI : Algebra Γ(X, V) Γ(Y, W) := (g.appLE V W hWle).hom.toAlgebra
245	  letI : Module Γ(X, V) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj G, W) :=
246	    Module.compHom _ (g.appLE V W hWle).hom
247	  obtain ⟨⟨eqv, -⟩⟩ :=
248	    pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv g G hW hV hWle
249	  -- fiber rank through the equivalence, then base change
250	  have h1 : ((hW.primeIdealOf ⟨y, hyW⟩).asIdeal).fiberRank
251	      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj G, W) =
252	      ((hW.primeIdealOf ⟨y, hyW⟩).asIdeal).fiberRank
253	        (TensorProduct Γ(X, V) Γ(Y, W) Γ(G, V)) :=
254	    Ideal.fiberRank_congr _ eqv.symm
255	  have h2 := Ideal.fiberRank_baseChange (R := Γ(X, V)) (A := Γ(Y, W))
256	    Γ(G, V) ((hW.primeIdealOf ⟨y, hyW⟩).asIdeal)
257	  -- identify the contracted prime with the prime of the image point
258	  have h3 : ((hW.primeIdealOf ⟨y, hyW⟩).asIdeal).comap
259	      (algebraMap Γ(X, V) Γ(Y, W)) =
260	      (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨g y, hgyV⟩).asIdeal := by
261	    have h4 := IsAffineOpen.comap_primeIdealOf_appLE (f := g)
262	      V hV W hW hWle hyW
263	    have h5 : algebraMap Γ(X, V) Γ(Y, W) = (g.appLE V W hWle).hom := rfl
264	    rw [h5]
265	    exact congrArg PrimeSpectrum.asIdeal h4
266	  show ((hW.primeIdealOf ⟨y, hyW⟩).asIdeal).fiberRank
267	      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj G, W) =
268	    ((hV.primeIdealOf ⟨g y, hgyV⟩).asIdeal).fiberRank Γ(G, V)
269	  rw [h1, h2]
270	  exact Ideal.fiberRank_congr_ideal h3
271	
272	end PointRankPullback
273	
274	/-! ## §3 Stage D1: unique factorization through the stratum -/
275	
276	section StageD1
277	
278	variable {X T : Scheme.{u}} (G : X.Modules) [G.IsQuasicoherent] {e : ℕ}
279	
280	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
281	/-- **The kernel bound** (the hard half of Nitsure's universal property,
282	`n = 0`, [Nitsure §4]: "`f^*𝓕` is locally free of rank `e` iff `f^*ψ = 0`
283	iff `f` factors through `V_e`"): if the pullback of `G` along `q : T ⟶ X`
284	is flat over `T` of constant point rank `e`, then the strata ideal sheaf is
285	contained in the kernel of `q` — every local relation matrix pulls back to
286	the zero matrix, so its entries die in `Γ(T, ·)`. -/
287	theorem strataData_le_ker (hcov : ChartsCover G e) (q : T ⟶ X)
288	    (hflat : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 T)
289	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj G))
290	    (hrank : ∀ t : T,
291	      haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom q G ‹_›
292	      pointRank T ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj G) t = e) :
293	    strataData G e hcov ≤ q.ker := by
294	  haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom q G ‹_›
295	  refine Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_ofIdeals_iff.mpr fun U => ?_
296	  intro r hr
297	  rw [RingHom.mem_ker]
298	  -- a finite family of `e`-presentation charts covering `U`
299	  obtain ⟨N, Vc, mmc, Pc, hVcU, hcovU⟩ :=
300	    ChartsCover.exists_finite_charts G hcov U
301	  -- the pulled-back section dies on an affine neighbourhood of each point
302	  apply T.IsSheaf.section_ext fun t ht => ?_
303	  obtain ⟨i, hti⟩ := hcovU (q.base t) ht
304	  have htVc : t ∈ q ⁻¹ᵁ (Vc i).1 := hti
305	  obtain ⟨W, hW, htW, hWle⟩ :=
306	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (x := t) (U := q ⁻¹ᵁ (Vc i).1) htVc
307	  have hV : W ≤ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1 := fun y hy => hVcU i (hWle hy)
308	  refine ⟨W, hV, htW, ?_⟩
309	  -- the relation matrix of the chart pulls back to zero on `W`
310	  haveI hFlatW : Module.Flat Γ(T, W)
311	      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj G, W) :=
312	    flat_sections_of_coherentSheafFlat_id hflat hW
313	  obtain ⟨P', hP'⟩ := exists_matrixPresentation_pullback_sections q G
314	    (Vc i).2 hW hWle (Pc i)
315	  haveI : Module.Finite Γ(T, W) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj G, W) :=
316	    Module.Finite.of_surjective P'.proj P'.surjective_proj
317	  have h0 : P'.relMatrix = 0 :=
318	    P'.relMatrix_eq_zero_of_flat fun p =>
319	      (rankAtStalk_sections_eq_pointRank _ hW p).trans (hrank _)
320	  -- hence the entry ideal is killed by `q.appLE (Vc i) W`
321	  letI : Algebra Γ(X, (Vc i).1) Γ(T, W) := (q.appLE (Vc i).1 W hWle).hom.toAlgebra
322	  have hker : (Pc i).entryIdeal ≤
323	      RingHom.ker (algebraMap Γ(X, (Vc i).1) Γ(T, W)) :=
324	    (_root_.Module.MatrixPresentation.relMatrix_map_eq_zero_iff
325	      (A := Γ(T, W))).mp (hP' ▸ h0)
326	  -- the strata-ideal condition at the chart
327	  have hmem : X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op r ∈ (Pc i).entryIdeal :=
328	    (mem_strataIdeal_iff G).mp hr (Vc i) (hVcU i) (mmc i) (Pc i)
329	  have hzero : (q.appLE (Vc i).1 W hWle).hom
330	      (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op r) = 0 :=
331	    RingHom.mem_ker.mp (hker hmem)
332	  -- assemble: restriction of `q.app U r` to `W` is `q.appLE (Vc i) W` of
333	  -- the restricted section
334	  have hres : (T.presheaf.map (homOfLE hV).op).hom ((q.app U.1).hom r) =
335	      (q.appLE U.1 W hV).hom r := rfl
336	  show (T.presheaf.map (homOfLE hV).op).hom ((q.app U.1).hom r) =
337	    (T.presheaf.map (homOfLE hV).op).hom 0
338	  rw [map_zero, hres, ← appLE_base_res_apply' q (hVcU i) hWle hV r, hzero]
339	
340	/-- **Unique factorization through the rank-`e` stratum** [Nitsure §4,
341	part (ii), `n = 0` case]: a morphism `q : T ⟶ X` whose pulled-back module
342	is flat over `T` of constant point rank `e` factors uniquely through the
343	closed immersion of the rank-`e` stratum. -/
344	theorem existsUnique_stratumLift (hcov : ChartsCover G e) (q : T ⟶ X)
345	    (hflat : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 T)
346	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj G))
347	    (hrank : ∀ t : T,
348	      haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom q G ‹_›
349	      pointRank T ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj G) t = e) :
350	    ∃! l : T ⟶ stratum G e hcov, l ≫ stratumι G e hcov = q := by
351	  have hker : (stratumι G e hcov).ker ≤ q.ker := by
352	    have h1 : (stratumι G e hcov).ker = strataData G e hcov :=
353	      Scheme.IdealSheafData.ker_subschemeι (strataData G e hcov)
354	    rw [h1]
355	    exact strataData_le_ker G hcov q hflat hrank
356	  refine ⟨IsClosedImmersion.lift (stratumι G e hcov) q hker,
357	    IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac _ _ hker, fun l' hl' => ?_⟩
358	  rw [← cancel_mono (stratumι G e hcov), hl',
359	    IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac]
360	
361	end StageD1
362	
363	/-! ## §4 Stage D2: the stratum pullback is flat -/
364	
365	section StageD2
366	
367	variable {X : Scheme.{u}} (G : X.Modules) [G.IsQuasicoherent] {e : ℕ}
368	
369	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
370	/-- **The rank-`e` stratum flattens `G`** [Nitsure §4, `n = 0`: on `V_e`
371	the pulled-back presentation has vanishing relation matrix, so `𝓕|_{V_e}`
372	is free of rank `e`]: the pullback of `G` along the stratum immersion is
373	flat over the stratum. -/
374	theorem coherentSheafFlat_stratum (hcov : ChartsCover G e) :
375	    Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 (stratum G e hcov))
376	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (stratumι G e hcov)).obj G) := by
377	  haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom (stratumι G e hcov) G ‹_›
378	  -- the chart preimages cover the stratum
379	  have hcover : ∀ z : stratum G e hcov,
380	      ∃ j : {V : X.affineOpens // IsPresentationChart G e V},
381	        z ∈ stratumι G e hcov ⁻¹ᵁ j.1.1 := by
382	    intro z
383	    obtain ⟨V, hzV, hchart⟩ := hcov ((stratumι G e hcov).base z)
384	    exact ⟨⟨V, hchart⟩, hzV⟩
385	  -- flat sections on each chart preimage: the pulled-back presentation
386	  -- has vanishing relation matrix, so the section module is free
387	  have hflatj : ∀ j : {V : X.affineOpens // IsPresentationChart G e V},
388	      Module.Flat Γ(stratum G e hcov, stratumι G e hcov ⁻¹ᵁ j.1.1)
389	        Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (stratumι G e hcov)).obj G,
390	          stratumι G e hcov ⁻¹ᵁ j.1.1) := by
391	    intro j
392	    obtain ⟨mm, ⟨P⟩⟩ := j.2
393	    obtain ⟨P', hP'⟩ := exists_matrixPresentation_pullback_sections
394	      (stratumι G e hcov) G j.1.2 (j.1.2.preimage (stratumι G e hcov))
395	      le_rfl P
396	    have h0 : P'.relMatrix = 0 := by
397	      rw [hP']
398	      ext a b
399	      simp only [Matrix.map_apply, Matrix.zero_apply]
400	      -- the entry lies in the strata ideal, the kernel of `stratumι.app`
401	      have hmem : ((stratumι G e hcov).app j.1.1).hom (P.relMatrix a b) = 0 := by
402	        have h1 : P.relMatrix a b ∈
403	            RingHom.ker (((strataData G e hcov).subschemeι.app j.1.1).hom) := by
404	          rw [Scheme.IdealSheafData.ker_subschemeι_app]
405	          show P.relMatrix a b ∈ strataIdeal G e j.1
406	          rw [strataIdeal_eq_entryIdeal G P]
407	          exact P.relMatrix_mem_entryIdeal a b
408	        exact RingHom.mem_ker.mp h1
409	      -- `appLE` at `le_rfl` is `app` followed by the trivial restriction
410	      show ((stratumι G e hcov).appLE j.1.1
411	        (stratumι G e hcov ⁻¹ᵁ j.1.1) le_rfl).hom (P.relMatrix a b) = 0
412	      have happ : ((stratumι G e hcov).appLE j.1.1
413	          (stratumι G e hcov ⁻¹ᵁ j.1.1) le_rfl).hom (P.relMatrix a b) =
414	          ((stratum G e hcov).presheaf.map (homOfLE le_rfl).op).hom
415	            (((stratumι G e hcov).app j.1.1).hom (P.relMatrix a b)) := rfl
416	      rw [happ, hmem, map_zero]
417	    haveI := P'.free_of_relMatrix_eq_zero h0
418	    infer_instance
419	  intro U hU V hV eV
420	  exact coherentSheafFlat_id_of_charts
421	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (stratumι G e hcov)).obj G)
422	    (fun j : {V : X.affineOpens // IsPresentationChart G e V} =>
423	      stratumι G e hcov ⁻¹ᵁ j.1.1)
424	    (fun j => j.1.2.preimage (stratumι G e hcov)) hcover hflatj hU hV eV
425	
426	end StageD2
427	
428	/-! ## §5 Stage E: chart supply and the chart locus (noetherian base) -/
429	
430	section ChartSupply
431	
432	/-- Any finitely presented module admits a matrix presentation (the
433	generators of the finitely generated kernel of a finite free surjection
434	are the columns of the relation matrix). -/
435	theorem _root_.Module.FinitePresentation.exists_matrixPresentation
436	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
437	    [Module.FinitePresentation R M] :
438	    ∃ (ee mm : ℕ), Nonempty (MatrixPresentation R M ee mm) := by
439	  classical
440	  obtain ⟨n, K, eqv, hK⟩ :=
441	    Module.FinitePresentation.exists_fin R M
442	  obtain ⟨T, hT⟩ := hK
443	  refine ⟨n, T.card, ⟨MatrixPresentation.congr (N := M) {
444	    relMatrix := Matrix.of fun i j ↦ (T.equivFin.symm j : Fin n → R) i
445	    proj := K.mkQ
446	    surjective_proj := Submodule.mkQ_surjective K
447	    exact_mulVecLin_proj := ?_ } eqv.symm⟩⟩
448	  rw [LinearMap.exact_iff, Submodule.ker_mkQ, ← hT]
449	  apply le_antisymm
450	  · -- each generator is a column of the matrix
451	    rw [Submodule.span_le]
452	    intro t ht
453	    obtain ⟨j, hj⟩ := T.equivFin.symm.surjective ⟨t, ht⟩
454	    refine ⟨Pi.single j 1, ?_⟩
455	    funext i
456	    simp only [Matrix.mulVecLin_apply, Matrix.mulVec, Matrix.of_apply]
457	    rw [dotProduct_single, mul_one, hj]
458	  · -- every matrix-vector product is a combination of the columns
459	    rintro _ ⟨x, rfl⟩
460	    have hcol : (Matrix.of fun i j ↦ (T.equivFin.symm j : Fin n → R) i).mulVecLin x =
461	        ∑ j, x j • ((T.equivFin.symm j : Fin n → R)) := by
462	      funext i
463	      simp [Matrix.mulVec, dotProduct, Finset.sum_apply, mul_comm]
464	    rw [hcol]
465	    exact Submodule.sum_mem _ fun j _ ↦ Submodule.smul_mem _ _
466	      (Submodule.subset_span (T.equivFin.symm j).2)
467	
468	variable {S : Scheme.{u}}
469	
470	-- the `Γ(S, U)`-module structure on sections over a basic open, from
471	-- `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.EntryIdealStratum` (declared there as local
472	-- instances)
473	attribute [local instance] moduleSectionBasicOpen isScalarTowerSectionBasicOpen
474	
475	/-- **Chart supply at every point** ([Nitsure §4], Nakayama prolongation of
476	a fibre basis; the geometric form of
477	`Module.FinitePresentation.exists_matrixPresentation_of_isLocalizedModule`):
478	on a locally noetherian scheme, every point of a finitely presented module
479	sheaf has an `e`-presentation chart around it with `e` its own point
480	rank. -/
481	theorem exists_presentationChart_mem [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
482	    (F : S.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] (s : S) :
483	    ∃ V : S.affineOpens, s ∈ V.1 ∧
484	      IsPresentationChart F (pointRank S F s) V := by
485	  obtain ⟨V, hV, hsV, -, hfin⟩ := exists_affine_finite_sections_nhds F s ⊤ trivial
486	  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Γ(S, V) :=
487	    IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian ⟨V, hV⟩
488	  haveI : Module.Finite Γ(S, V) Γ(F, V) := hfin
489	  haveI : Module.FinitePresentation Γ(S, V) Γ(F, V) :=
490	    Module.finitePresentation_of_finite _ _
491	  obtain ⟨g, hgp, hloc⟩ :=
492	    Module.FinitePresentation.exists_matrixPresentation_of_isLocalizedModule
493	      (M := Γ(F, V)) (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨s, hsV⟩).asIdeal
494	  haveI := hV.isLocalization_basicOpen g
495	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen F hV g
496	  obtain ⟨mm, ⟨P⟩⟩ := hloc Γ(S, S.basicOpen g) Γ(F, S.basicOpen g)
497	    (restrictBasicOpenₗ F g)
498	  have hrank : ((hV.primeIdealOf ⟨s, hsV⟩).asIdeal).fiberRank Γ(F, V) =
499	      pointRank S F s :=
500	    (pointRank_eq_chartFiberRank F (V := ⟨V, hV⟩) s hsV).symm
501	  refine ⟨S.affineBasicOpen (U := ⟨V, hV⟩) g, ?_, mm, ⟨hrank ▸ P⟩⟩
502	  exact (hV.mem_basicOpen_iff_notMem_primeIdealOf g ⟨s, hsV⟩).mpr hgp
503	
504	variable (F : S.Modules) (e : ℕ)
505	
506	/-- The **chart locus**: the union of all `e`-presentation charts — the
507	open locus on which the rank-`e` stratum is a closed subscheme
508	[Nitsure §4: the ambient open `V` of the local construction]. -/
509	noncomputable def chartLocus : S.Opens :=
510	  ⨆ V : {V : S.affineOpens // IsPresentationChart F e V}, V.1.1
511	
512	/-- Points of point rank exactly `e` lie in the chart locus. -/
513	theorem mem_chartLocus_of_pointRank_eq [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
514	    [F.IsFinitePresentation] {s : S} (h : pointRank S F s = e) :
515	    s ∈ chartLocus F e := by
516	  obtain ⟨V, hsV, hchart⟩ := exists_presentationChart_mem F s
517	  exact TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr ⟨⟨V, h ▸ hchart⟩, hsV⟩
518	
519	/-- Points of the chart locus have point rank at most `e` (an
520	`e`-generator presentation bounds every fibre dimension). -/
521	theorem pointRank_le_of_mem_chartLocus [F.IsQuasicoherent] {s : S}
522	    (h : s ∈ chartLocus F e) : pointRank S F s ≤ e := by
523	  obtain ⟨⟨V, hchart⟩, hsV⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp h
524	  obtain ⟨mm, ⟨P⟩⟩ := hchart
525	  rw [pointRank_eq_chartFiberRank F (V := V) s hsV]
526	  exact P.fiberRank_le _
527	
528	end ChartSupply

532	section FlatPullback
533	
534	/-- **Flatness is stable under arbitrary pullback**: if `G` is flat over
535	`T` (via `𝟙 T`), then `g^*G` is flat over `T'` for any morphism
536	`g : T' ⟶ T` — affine-locally the sections are a base change of a flat
537	module. -/
538	theorem coherentSheafFlat_id_pullback {T' T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T' ⟶ T)
539	    (G : T.Modules) [G.IsQuasicoherent]
540	    (h : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 T) G) :
541	    Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 T') ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj G) := by
542	  haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom g G ‹_›
543	  -- cover `T'` by affine opens lying over affine opens of `T`
544	  set J := {p : T'.Opens × T.Opens //
545	    IsAffineOpen p.1 ∧ IsAffineOpen p.2 ∧ p.1 ≤ g ⁻¹ᵁ p.2} with hJ
546	  have hcover : ∀ t' : T', ∃ j : J, t' ∈ j.1.1 := by
547	    intro t'
548	    obtain ⟨V, hV, hgt', -⟩ :=
549	      exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (x := g.base t') (U := ⊤) trivial
550	    have ht' : t' ∈ g ⁻¹ᵁ V := hgt'
551	    obtain ⟨W, hW, htW, hWle⟩ :=
552	      exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (x := t') (U := g ⁻¹ᵁ V) ht'
553	    exact ⟨⟨(W, V), hW, hV, hWle⟩, htW⟩
554	  have hflatj : ∀ j : J, Module.Flat Γ(T', j.1.1)
555	      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj G, j.1.1) := by
556	    intro ⟨⟨W, V⟩, hW, hV, hle⟩
557	    haveI : Module.Flat Γ(T, V) Γ(G, V) :=
558	      flat_sections_of_coherentSheafFlat_id h hV
559	    letI : Algebra Γ(T, V) Γ(T', W) := (g.appLE V W hle).hom.toAlgebra
560	    letI : Module Γ(T, V) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj G, W) :=
561	      Module.compHom _ (g.appLE V W hle).hom
562	    obtain ⟨⟨eqv, -⟩⟩ :=
563	      pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv g G hW hV hle
564	    exact Module.Flat.of_linearEquiv eqv.symm
565	  intro U hU V hV eV
566	  exact flat_section_of_affine_cover (𝟙 T') ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj G)
567	    (fun j : J => j.1.1) (fun j => j.2.1) (fun j : J => j.1.1) (fun j => j.2.1)
568	    (fun j => le_refl _) hcover
569	    (fun j => (flat_compHom_congr
570	      ((𝟙 T' : T' ⟶ T').appLE j.1.1 j.1.1 (le_refl _)).hom
571	      (id_appLE_apply _)).mpr (hflatj j)) hU hV eV
572	
573	end FlatPullback

575	section RankStratum
576	
577	variable {S : Scheme.{u}} (F : S.Modules) (e : ℕ) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
578	
579	/-- The restriction of `F` to the chart locus is chart-covered: the charts
580	of `S` inside the chart locus pull back to charts of the open subscheme
581	(the pullback of an `e`-generator presentation is an `e`-generator
582	presentation). -/
583	theorem chartsCover_chartLocus :
584	    ChartsCover ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).ι).obj F) e := by
585	  intro x
586	  have hx : ((chartLocus F e).ι.base x) ∈ chartLocus F e := by
587	    have h0 : ((chartLocus F e).ι.base x) ∈ Set.range (chartLocus F e).ι :=
588	      Set.mem_range_self x
589	    rwa [Scheme.Opens.range_ι] at h0
590	  obtain ⟨⟨V, hchart⟩, hxV⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp hx
591	  have hVle : V.1 ≤ (chartLocus F e).ι.opensRange := by
592	    rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
593	    exact le_iSup
594	      (fun W : {V : S.affineOpens // IsPresentationChart F e V} => W.1.1)
595	      ⟨V, hchart⟩
596	  have hpre : IsAffineOpen ((chartLocus F e).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V.1) :=
597	    V.2.preimage_of_isOpenImmersion (chartLocus F e).ι hVle
598	  refine ⟨⟨(chartLocus F e).ι ⁻¹ᵁ V.1, hpre⟩, hxV, ?_⟩
599	  obtain ⟨mm, ⟨P⟩⟩ := hchart
600	  obtain ⟨P', -⟩ := exists_matrixPresentation_pullback_sections
601	    (chartLocus F e).ι F V.2 hpre le_rfl P
602	  exact ⟨mm, ⟨P'⟩⟩
603	
604	/-- The **rank-`e` stratum of `F`**: the canonical closed subscheme of the
605	chart locus cut out by the entry ideals [Nitsure §4, `n = 0`: the closed
606	subscheme `V_e ⊆ V`]. -/
607	noncomputable def rankStratum : Scheme.{u} :=
608	  haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom (chartLocus F e).ι F ‹_›
609	  stratum ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).ι).obj F) e
610	    (chartsCover_chartLocus F e)
611	
612	/-- The locally closed immersion of the rank-`e` stratum into the base. -/
613	noncomputable def rankStratumι : rankStratum F e ⟶ S :=
614	  haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom (chartLocus F e).ι F ‹_›
615	  stratumι ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).ι).obj F) e
616	    (chartsCover_chartLocus F e) ≫ (chartLocus F e).ι
617	
618	instance : IsImmersion (rankStratumι F e) := by
619	  haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom (chartLocus F e).ι F ‹_›
620	  show IsImmersion
621	    (stratumι ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).ι).obj F) e
622	      (chartsCover_chartLocus F e) ≫ (chartLocus F e).ι)
623	  infer_instance
624	
625	/-- **The support of the rank-`e` stratum is the rank-`e` locus**
626	[Nitsure §4, part (i)]. -/
627	theorem mem_range_rankStratumι_iff [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
628	    [F.IsFinitePresentation] (s : S) :
629	    s ∈ Set.range (rankStratumι F e).base ↔ pointRank S F s = e := by
630	  haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom (chartLocus F e).ι F ‹_›
631	  constructor
632	  · rintro ⟨z, rfl⟩
633	    have hz : (rankStratumι F e).base z = ((chartLocus F e).ι.base)
634	        ((stratumι ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).ι).obj F) e
635	          (chartsCover_chartLocus F e)).base z) := rfl
636	    rw [hz, ← pointRank_pullback (chartLocus F e).ι F]
637	    exact (mem_range_stratumι_iff _ (chartsCover_chartLocus F e) _).mp
638	      ⟨z, rfl⟩
639	  · intro h
640	    have hs : s ∈ chartLocus F e := mem_chartLocus_of_pointRank_eq F e h
641	    have hs' : s ∈ Set.range (chartLocus F e).ι := by
642	      rwa [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
643	    obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := hs'
644	    have hrx : pointRank ((chartLocus F e) : Scheme.{u})
645	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).ι).obj F) x = e := by
646	      rw [pointRank_pullback (chartLocus F e).ι F x, hx]
647	      exact h
648	    obtain ⟨z, hz⟩ := (mem_range_stratumι_iff _
649	      (chartsCover_chartLocus F e) x).mpr hrx
650	    refine ⟨z, ?_⟩
651	    show ((chartLocus F e).ι.base)
652	      ((stratumι ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).ι).obj F) e
653	        (chartsCover_chartLocus F e)).base z) = s
654	    rw [show ((stratumι ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).ι).obj F) e
655	      (chartsCover_chartLocus F e)).base z) = x from hz, hx]
656	
657	/-- **The rank-`e` stratum flattens `F`** [Nitsure §4, part (i)]: the
658	pullback of `F` along the stratum immersion is flat over the stratum. -/
659	theorem coherentSheafFlat_rankStratum :
660	    Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 (rankStratum F e))
661	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (rankStratumι F e)).obj F) := by
662	  haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom (chartLocus F e).ι F ‹_›
663	  intro U hU V hV eV
664	  exact coherentSheafFlat_of_iso (𝟙 (rankStratum F e))
665	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp
666	      (stratumι ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).ι).obj F) e
667	        (chartsCover_chartLocus F e)) (chartLocus F e).ι).app F)
668	    (coherentSheafFlat_stratum
669	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).ι).obj F)
670	      (chartsCover_chartLocus F e)) hU hV eV
671	
672	end RankStratum

674	/-! ## §7 Stage E: rank bound and openness of the rank fibers -/
675	
676	section RankBound
677	
678	variable {S : Scheme.{u}}
679	
680	/-- **The point rank is bounded on a noetherian scheme**: finitely many
681	presentation charts cover `S` by quasi-compactness, and each caps the rank
682	by its generator count. -/
683	theorem exists_pointRank_le [IsNoetherian S] (F : S.Modules)
684	    [F.IsFinitePresentation] : ∃ N : ℕ, ∀ s : S, pointRank S F s ≤ N := by
685	  classical
686	  choose Vc hVc hchart using exists_presentationChart_mem (S := S) F
687	  have hcov : (Set.univ : Set S) ⊆ ⋃ s : S, ((Vc s).1 : Set S) := fun x _ =>
688	    Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ⟨x, hVc x⟩
689	  obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := isCompact_univ.elim_finite_subcover
690	    (fun s : S => ((Vc s).1 : Set S)) (fun s => (Vc s).1.isOpen) hcov
691	  refine ⟨t.sup fun s => pointRank S F s, fun x => ?_⟩
692	  obtain ⟨s, hst, hxs⟩ := Set.mem_iUnion₂.mp (ht (Set.mem_univ x))
693	  calc pointRank S F x ≤ pointRank S F s := by
694	        obtain ⟨mm, ⟨P⟩⟩ := hchart s
695	        rw [pointRank_eq_chartFiberRank F (V := Vc s) x hxs]
696	        exact P.fiberRank_le _
697	    _ ≤ t.sup (fun s => pointRank S F s) := Finset.le_sup hst
698	
699	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
700	/-- **The rank fibers of a flat pullback are open** (local constancy of the
701	rank of a flat, finitely presented module, geometrized through `fromSpec`):
702	if `φ^*F` is flat over `T`, the locus where its point rank equals `e` is
703	open in `T`. -/
704	theorem isOpen_pointRank_pullback_eq {T : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
705	    (F : S.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] (φ : T ⟶ S)
706	    (hflat : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 T)
707	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj F)) (e : ℕ) :
708	    haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom φ F inferInstance
709	    IsOpen {t : T | pointRank T ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj F) t = e} := by
710	  haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom φ F inferInstance
711	  rw [isOpen_iff_forall_mem_open]
712	  intro t₀ ht₀
713	  -- affine charts: `V ∋ φ t₀` with finitely presented sections, `W ∋ t₀`
714	  obtain ⟨V, hV, hφt, -, hfin⟩ :=
715	    exists_affine_finite_sections_nhds F (φ.base t₀) ⊤ trivial
716	  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Γ(S, V) :=
717	    IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian ⟨V, hV⟩
718	  haveI : Module.Finite Γ(S, V) Γ(F, V) := hfin
719	  haveI : Module.FinitePresentation Γ(S, V) Γ(F, V) :=
720	    Module.finitePresentation_of_finite _ _
721	  have ht' : t₀ ∈ φ ⁻¹ᵁ V := hφt
722	  obtain ⟨W, hW, htW, hWle⟩ :=
723	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (x := t₀) (U := φ ⁻¹ᵁ V) ht'
724	  -- section module over `W`: flat and finitely presented
725	  haveI : Module.Flat Γ(T, W) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj F, W) :=
726	    flat_sections_of_coherentSheafFlat_id hflat hW
727	  obtain ⟨ee, mm, ⟨P⟩⟩ := Module.FinitePresentation.exists_matrixPresentation
728	    (R := Γ(S, V)) (M := Γ(F, V))
729	  obtain ⟨P', -⟩ := exists_matrixPresentation_pullback_sections φ F hV hW hWle P
730	  haveI : Module.FinitePresentation Γ(T, W)
731	      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj F, W) := P'.finitePresentation
732	  -- transport the locally constant stalk rank through `fromSpec`
733	  have hlc := Module.isLocallyConstant_rankAtStalk
734	    (R := Γ(T, W)) (M := Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj F, W))
735	  refine ⟨hW.fromSpec.base ''
736	    (Module.rankAtStalk Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj F, W) ⁻¹' {e}),
737	    ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
738	  · rintro _ ⟨p, hp, rfl⟩
739	    show pointRank T ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj F)
740	      (hW.fromSpec.base p) = e
741	    rw [← rankAtStalk_sections_eq_pointRank
742	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj F) hW p]
743	    exact hp
744	  · exact hW.fromSpec.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap _ (hlc.isOpen_fiber e)
745	  · refine ⟨hW.primeIdealOf ⟨t₀, htW⟩, ?_, ?_⟩
746	    · show Module.rankAtStalk Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj F, W)
747	        (hW.primeIdealOf ⟨t₀, htW⟩) = e
748	      rw [rankAtStalk_sections_eq_pointRank
749	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj F) hW]
750	      have hfs : (hW.fromSpec.base (hW.primeIdealOf ⟨t₀, htW⟩) : T) = t₀ :=
751	        hW.fromSpec_primeIdealOf ⟨t₀, htW⟩
752	      rw [hfs]
753	      exact ht₀
754	    · exact hW.fromSpec_primeIdealOf ⟨t₀, htW⟩
755	
756	end RankBound

758	/-! ## §8 Stage E: the universal factorization through a single stratum -/
759	
760	section FactorRankStratum
761	
762	variable {S : Scheme.{u}} (F : S.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
763	
764	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
765	/-- **Unique factorization through the rank-`e` stratum of the base**
766	[Nitsure §4, part (ii), `n = 0`, single-rank case]: a morphism `φ : T ⟶ S`
767	whose pulled-back module is flat with *constant* point rank `e` (measured
768	on the base) factors uniquely through the locally closed immersion
769	`rankStratumι`. -/
770	theorem existsUnique_factor_rankStratum [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
771	    [F.IsFinitePresentation] (e : ℕ) {T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T ⟶ S)
772	    (hflat : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 T)
773	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj F))
774	    (hrank : ∀ t : T, pointRank S F (φ.base t) = e) :
775	    ∃! l : T ⟶ rankStratum F e, l ≫ rankStratumι F e = φ := by
776	  haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom (chartLocus F e).ι F inferInstance
777	  -- `φ` lands in the chart locus
778	  have hrange : Set.range φ.base ⊆ Set.range (chartLocus F e).ι.base := by
779	    rintro _ ⟨t, rfl⟩
780	    have h1 : φ.base t ∈ chartLocus F e :=
781	      mem_chartLocus_of_pointRank_eq F e (hrank t)
782	    have h2 : (chartLocus F e : Set S) ⊆ Set.range (chartLocus F e).ι.base := by
783	      rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
784	    exact h2 h1
785	  set q : T ⟶ (chartLocus F e : Scheme.{u}) :=
786	    IsOpenImmersion.lift (chartLocus F e).ι φ hrange with hq
787	  have hqfac : q ≫ (chartLocus F e).ι = φ :=
788	    IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac (chartLocus F e).ι φ hrange
789	  -- flatness of the pullback along `q`
790	  have hflat_q : Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (𝟙 T)
791	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj
792	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).ι).obj F)) := by
793	    intro U hU V hV eV
794	    exact coherentSheafFlat_of_iso (𝟙 T)
795	      (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp q (chartLocus F e).ι).app F ≪≫
796	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hqfac).app F).symm) hflat hU hV eV
797	  -- constant rank of the pullback along `q`
798	  have hrank_q : ∀ t : T,
799	      haveI := pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom q
800	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).ι).obj F) ‹_›
801	      pointRank T ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj
802	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).ι).obj F)) t = e := by
803	    intro t
804	    rw [pointRank_pullback q ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).ι).obj F) t,
805	      pointRank_pullback (chartLocus F e).ι F]
806	    have hpt : (chartLocus F e).ι.base (q.base t) = φ.base t := by
807	      have := congrArg (fun m : T ⟶ S => m.base t) hqfac
808	      simpa using this
809	    rw [hpt]
810	    exact hrank t
811	  obtain ⟨l, hl, hluniq⟩ := existsUnique_stratumLift
812	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).ι).obj F)
813	    (chartsCover_chartLocus F e) q hflat_q hrank_q
814	  refine ⟨l, ?_, ?_⟩
815	  · show l ≫ (stratumι ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).ι).obj F) e
816	      (chartsCover_chartLocus F e) ≫ (chartLocus F e).ι) = φ
817	    rw [← Category.assoc, hl, hqfac]
818	  · intro l' hl'
819	    refine hluniq l' ?_
820	    show l' ≫ stratumι ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).ι).obj F) e
821	      (chartsCover_chartLocus F e) = q
822	    rw [← cancel_mono (chartLocus F e).ι, Category.assoc]
823	    show l' ≫ (stratumι ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartLocus F e).ι).obj F) e
824	      (chartsCover_chartLocus F e) ≫ (chartLocus F e).ι) = q ≫ (chartLocus F e).ι
825	    rw [hqfac]
826	    exact hl'
827	
828	end FactorRankStratum
829	
830	end Scheme.Modules

832	/-! ## §9 The universal property of the flat-locus stratification -/
833	
834	section UniversalTheorem
835	
836	open Scheme.Modules Limits
837	
838	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in

839	/-- **Universal property of the flat-locus stratification (the `n = 0`
840	flattening stratification)** [Nitsure §4, special case, parts (i) + (ii)].
841	
842	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` a coherent `𝓞_S`-module there is a *finite*
843	locally-closed stratification `{S_f}` of `S` — immersions, set-theoretically
844	covering `|S|`, pairwise disjoint, `𝓕|_{S_f}` flat over `S_f` — such that,
845	writing `i : ∐ S_f ⟶ S` for `Sigma.desc` of the inclusions, every morphism
846	`φ : T ⟶ S` for which `φ^*𝓕` is flat over `T` factors *uniquely* through
847	`i`.
848	
849	Source: [Nitsure], §4, proof of the flattening-stratification theorem,
850	special case `n = 0` (Nakayama prolongation of a fibre basis to a local
851	presentation `𝓞_V^{⊕m} →ψ 𝓞_V^{⊕e} → 𝓕|_V → 0`, the closed subscheme
852	`V_e ⊆ V` cut out by the entry ideal of `ψ`, base change: `f^*𝓕` is locally
853	free of rank `e` iff `f^*ψ = 0` iff `f` factors through `V_e`; the local
854	strata glue by their universal property).  Flatness replaces
855	locally-free-of-rank-`e`: for finitely presented `𝓕` they agree, with the
856	rank decomposing `T` into clopen pieces.
857	
858	Statement repair (run 0010, T12 r7): the statement is specialized to
859	`π = 𝟙 S` — exactly Nitsure's special case, which his general-case proof
860	consumes; see the git history of
861	`AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GenericFlatnessGeometric` for the discussion.
862	
863	Proof (run 0010, T12): the strata are the canonical rank-`e` strata
864	`rankStratum F e` — closed subschemes of the open chart loci cut out by the
865	entry ideals (`AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.EntryIdealStratum`) — for
866	`e ≤ N` a rank bound (`exists_pointRank_le`, noetherian compactness).
867	Support, disjointness and covering are `mem_range_rankStratumι_iff`;
868	flatness over the strata is `coherentSheafFlat_rankStratum` (the pulled
869	back presentation has vanishing relation matrix).  For the universal
870	property, `T` decomposes into *open* rank pieces (local constancy of the
871	rank of a flat finitely presented module, `isOpen_pointRank_pullback_eq`),
872	giving a colimit cofan; each piece factors uniquely through its stratum
873	(`existsUnique_factor_rankStratum`, via `IsClosedImmersion.lift` and the
874	vanishing of the pulled-back relation matrices, `strataData_le_ker`), and
875	the factorizations assemble.  Uniqueness: any competing factorization
876	lands, piecewise, in the same summand (the summand ranges have distinct
877	ranks), where it agrees by the monomorphism property of immersions. -/
878	theorem flatLocusStratification_universal {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsNoetherian S]
879	    (F : S.Modules) [F.IsFinitePresentation] :
880	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S),

1826	\textbf{(ii) Universal property: } 
1827	Let $S'=\coprod S_f$ be the coproduct of the $S_f$, and let
1828	$i:S'\to S$ be the morphism induced by the inclusions
1829	$S_f\hra S$. Then the sheaf $i^*(\F)$ on $\PP^n_{S'}$ 
1830	is flat over $S'$. Moreover, $i:S'\to S$ has the universal property that 
1831	for any morphism $\varphi :T\to S$ the pullback $\varphi^*(\F)$ on
1832	$\PP^n_T$ is flat over $T$ if and only if $\varphi$ factors through
1833	$i:S'\to S$.  The subscheme $S_f$ is 
1834	uniquely determined by the polynomial $f$.
1835	
1836	\textbf{(iii) Closure of strata: } 
1837	Let the set $I$ of Hilbert polynomials be given a total 
1838	ordering, defined by putting $f<g$ whenever $f(n) < g(n)$ for all
1839	$n \gg  0$. Then the closure in $S$ of the subset $|S_f|$

1841	\end{theorem}

1844	\proof It is enough to prove the theorem for open subschemes of

1850	For any $s\in S$, the \textbf{fiber} $\F|_s$ of $\F$ over $s$
1851	will mean the pull-back of $\F$ to the subscheme
1852	$\spec \kappa(s)$, where $\kappa(s)$ is the residue field at $s$. 
1853	(This is obtained by tensoring the stalk of $\F$ at $s$ with the
1854	residue field at $s$, both regarded as ${\mathcal O}_{S,s}$-modules.)
1855	The Hilbert polynomial of the 
1856	restriction of $\F$ to the fiber over $s$ is the degree $0$ 
1857	polynomial $e \in \Q[\lambda]$, where $e = \dim_{\kappa(s)}\F|_s$.

1859	By Nakayama lemma, any basis of $\F|_s$ prolongs to a neighbourhood $U$ of $s$

  Iter-188 Lane F-introduced \(\Sigma\)-pair packaging declaration that

  \(\Gamma(V,(g^*\mathcal N)) \;\simeq_{\Gamma(Y,U)}\; \Gamma(Y,U) \otimes
    \Gamma(N,V)\) AND its intertwining property with the canonical
  base-change map into a single \(\Sigma\)-struct returned via
  \texttt{Nonempty} for \texttt{IsBaseChange.of\_equiv} consumption.
  Concretely:
  \[
    \exists\, (f : \Gamma(V,(\text{pullback}\, g).\mathrm{obj}\,
                     \mathcal N) \;\simeq_{\Gamma(Y,U)}\;
                     \Gamma(X,V) \otimes_{\Gamma(Y,U)} \Gamma(N,V)),\;

  This packaging idiom keeps consumer signatures clean and is the project-

\begin{lemma}[Charts with finitely generated sections]
  \leanok
  \label{lem:affine_finite_sections_nhds}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.exists_affine_finite_sections_nhds}
  \uses{lem:module_finite_sections_qcdata}
  \textit{Source: Stacks~\href{https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/01PC}{01PC}.}
  Let \(\mathcal F\) be a module sheaf of finite presentation on a scheme
  \(X\). Every point \(x\) of every open \(O \subseteq X\) has an affine open
  neighbourhood \(V\) with \(x \in V \subseteq O\) such that
  \(\Gamma(\mathcal F, V)\) is a finite \(\Gamma(X, V)\)-module.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \leanok
  Choose a quasi-coherence datum \(q\) with finite presentations. The cover
  members \(q.X_i\) cover \(X\), so \(x\) lies in some \(q.X_i\); affine opens
  form a basis of the topology, so there is an affine
  \(V \subseteq O \cap q.X_i\) containing \(x\). Conclude by
  \cref{lem:module_finite_sections_qcdata}.
\end{proof}
